highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.111: Claude Opus 4.7 xhigh Effort Level Released
highlights.summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 with a new 'xhigh' effort level that sits between 'high' and 'max', letting you fine-tune the tradeoff between speed and intelligence. You can activate it using the /effort command, --effort flag, or directly in the model settings. Auto mode is also now available for Max subscribers using Opus 4.7.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.116: Faster /resume and MCP Startup
highlights.summary: The /resume command is now up to 67% faster on large sessions (40MB+) and handles sessions with many dead-fork entries more efficiently — a big win if you work on long-running projects. MCP startup time is also improved when multiple stdio servers are configured, as resource template listing is now deferred until you actually use an @-mention.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.113: Native Binary, Domain Blocking, and More
highlights.summary: Claude Code now spawns a native binary per platform instead of bundled JavaScript, which should improve startup performance and reliability. A new sandbox.network.deniedDomains setting lets you block specific domains even when a broader wildcard in allowedDomains would otherwise allow them — useful for tightening security in agentic workflows.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic's official announcement of Claude Opus 4.7 highlights its expanded 1M token context window and significant improvements in coding benchmarks over 4.6. If you use Claude Code for complex, multi-file or long-running coding tasks, this model upgrade is directly relevant to your daily workflow.

highlights.title: FULL Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners in 2026! (Step-By-Step)
highlights.summary: A comprehensive step-by-step video tutorial aimed at developers who are brand new to Claude Code in 2026. It covers setup, core commands, and practical use cases to get you productive quickly. This is a great starting point if you have never used Claude Code before.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute video designed to take you from basic familiarity to confident everyday use of Claude Code. It covers key workflows and time-saving techniques that intermediate users will find immediately applicable. A good watch if you want to level up without committing to a multi-hour course.

highlights.title: Claude Opus 4.7 Full Review: 1M Context + xhigh Thinking Mode (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: A Chinese-language deep-dive video testing Claude Opus 4.7's coding capabilities across front-end development, iOS native apps, open-source project PRs, and research paper implementation. The reviewer notes Opus 4.7 scores 10%+ higher than 4.6 on SWE-bench Pro and outperforms GPT 5.4 in coding tasks. If you want to see real-world agentic coding workflows with the new xhigh effort mode, this is a solid hands-on reference.

tips_of_the_day: Use /effort xhigh in Claude Code when tackling complex multi-step refactors or architecture decisions — it gives you more reasoning depth than 'high' without the full cost of 'max', making it a sweet spot for difficult but time-sensitive tasks.

If your Claude Code sessions are growing large and /resume feels slow, consider breaking long-running projects into focused sub-sessions. Smaller, well-scoped sessions resume faster and keep context more relevant — and with v2.1.116, even large sessions are now significantly quicker to restore.

When configuring MCP servers for your workflow, use sandbox.network.deniedDomains to explicitly block sensitive internal or third-party domains even if you have a broad wildcard in allowedDomains — this is a simple way to add a security safety net to agentic tasks that browse or call external services.
